metadata.summary: Reusable variant composition using cva() (class-variance-authority) and cn() for type-safe, conflict-free class merging in Tailwind components.

content.title: Tailwind Patterns with CVA
content.summary: Tailwind Patterns covers reusable variant composition on top of an existing Tailwind project using `cva()` (class-variance-authority) for type-safe variant definitions and `cn()` (clsx + tailwind-merge) for conflict-safe class merging. Every component has one `cva()` block next to it; variants describe intent (`tone="danger"`) not style (`color="red"`). Dynamic class interpolation (`bg-${color}-500`) is forbidden — JIT purges interpolated strings.

why.p: Raw utility strings copied across components defeat the utility-first value proposition. `cva()` turns variant logic into a typed API that agents can extend without introducing class conflicts or tailwind-merge collisions. The pattern also makes components friendly to visual regression: Storybook can matrix every variant automatically.

list.item: Existing Tailwind project needing reusable variant patterns (button, badge, card, input) without pulling in a UI library.
Type-safe class composition via `cva()` + `cn()` for AI-generated components.
Migrating ad-hoc utility soup into named components without giving up utility-first.
Building a private design system on top of Tailwind for a single product.
Project does not yet have Tailwind configured — start with the `tailwind` methodology first.
Static marketing site that ships once and is never refactored; raw utilities are fine.
Team prefers CSS Modules or styled-components — mixing paradigms doubles the surface area.

rule.statement: Define one `cva()` block per component, colocated in the component file. Never centralize variant definitions across multiple components in a shared variants file.
rule.rationale: Centralized variant files create invisible coupling. A change to a shared cva() block breaks components that import it. Colocation keeps the component self-contained and diffable.

rule.statement: Name variants by intent, not by style: `tone="danger"` not `color="red"`, `size="sm"` not `padding="small"`. Variant values must map to design tokens, not raw Tailwind classes.
rule.rationale: Intent names decouple component API from implementation. When the brand changes "danger" from red to orange, only the cva() mapping changes — callers are unaffected.

rule.statement: Never build Tailwind class names with template literals or string interpolation (`bg-${color}-500`). Use lookup objects with full literal class strings.
rule.rationale: Tailwind's JIT compiler scans for string literals. Interpolated strings are not statically analyzable — the class is purged from the output CSS and the component renders unstyled.

rule.statement: Always merge className overrides through `cn()` (clsx + tailwind-merge). Never pass raw className strings to JSX elements alongside cva() output.
rule.rationale: Without tailwind-merge, conflicting utility classes (e.g. `p-2 p-4`) resolve non-deterministically. cn() makes the last-wins rule explicit and predictable for agents.

rule.statement: Use Tailwind data-* selectors (`data-[state=open]:opacity-100`) instead of toggling class names imperatively via JavaScript. State drives data attributes, not class lists.
rule.rationale: Class toggling in JS creates a second source of truth. data-* selectors are declarative, visible in markup, and compatible with Radix UI's built-in state attributes.

rule.statement: Use `compoundVariants` only for overrides that apply when two or more specific variant values co-occur. Do not use them to define new base variants.
rule.rationale: Misusing compoundVariants as a variant slot bloats the cva() config and confuses type inference. New visual states deserve a first-class variant, not a compound override.

example.title: Button Component with CVA Variants
Input Component with State Variants
Card Compound Pattern
Dark Mode Using data-* Attributes

antipattern.reason: JIT scans for string literals only. Interpolated class names are purged from the output CSS. Use lookup objects with full class strings.

antipattern.description: Agents propose @apply chains in a shared stylesheet to abstract variants.
antipattern.reason: @apply in a global stylesheet defeats the per-component variant pattern. Variants belong in cva() next to the component. Reject @apply usage outside globals.css reset helpers.

antipattern.description: Agents add new colors to tailwind.config but forget the matching CSS variable in globals.css.
antipattern.reason: The build succeeds (TypeScript passes) but dark mode renders the wrong color. Every new token in config needs a corresponding --token-name entry in both :root and .dark blocks.

template.purpose: Full Button component with cva() variants (primary, secondary, outline, ghost, danger) and sizes.
Input component with label, error, hint, aria wiring, and cva() state variants.
CI guard: fail on dynamic Tailwind class interpolation.
